info.title: dotnet-tools-outdated
info.description: Checks whether any of installed .NET command-line tools is outdated.

options.name: --name
options.description: Check (and show) only one particular package

options.name: --format
options.description:
Output format. Valid values are xml, json, or table.
(Default: table)

options.name: --outPkgRegardlessState
options.description:
Otput the both up-to-date/outdated state packages for
the json and xml format. (The table format can show
only the outdated packages)

options.name: --noIndent
options.description: No indenttation (for the json and xml format)

options.name: --utf8
options.description:
Output with UTF-8 instead of the system default
encoding. (no bom)

options.name: --showStat
options.description:
Show statistics info row (sums of available and
outdated packages)

options.name: --globalToolsPath
options.description:
Use custom location of the globally installed .NET
tools
It checks globaly installed tools. It also checks locally installed tools, when being run from a folder of locally installed tools. The outdated global packages can be then re-installed by dotnet tool uninstall -g package_name followed by dotnet tool install -g package_name command. (For the locally installed packages, check the appropriate manifest file and use the above commands without the -g option)
